findingDetails.title: `--path` boundary docs omit the live `/tmp` test-fixture exception
findingDetails.evidence: The workflow and quick references state traversal outside the repository is rejected, while create.sh permits resolved targets under /tmp or TMPDIR for test fixtures before enforcing the repository boundary.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reword workflow references to state traversal is rejected and normal targets must stay inside the repository, with absolute /tmp/TMPDIR paths reserved for test fixtures.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same boundary wording appears in execution_methods.md and quick_reference.md; create.sh has one resolve_and_validate_create_target gate with a /tmp/TMPDIR exception before repo-boundary rejection.

findingDetails.title: Lazy research document contract cannot be rendered through the shared contract-doc helper path
findingDetails.evidence: The manifest exposes public lazy output research/research.md with template research.md.tmpl, while template-utils maps generic document names to manifest/${template_name}.tmpl except for phase spec.md; a generic resolver consumer trying to render the lazy doc by public name would look for templates/manifest/research/research.md.tmpl. Default scaffold excludes lazy docs and docs instruct direct research.md.tmpl rendering, so this is advisory.
findingDetails.recommendation: Include document-template mapping in the serialized resolver contract and have shell helpers consult manifest.documents[doc].template, or add an explicit research/research.md -> research.md.tmpl mapping with a regression test for lazy doc rendering by public document name.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Manifest documents research/research.md -> research.md.tmpl; lazyAddonDocs expose research/research.md across Levels 1/2/3/3+; template-utils only special-cases phase spec.md and otherwise appends .tmpl to the public doc path; level_contract_docs_from_json excludes lazy docs from default scaffold.

Existing renderer and resolver tests were read for gate expression behavior, CLI output naming, Level contract serialization, and lazy-doc exposure coverage.
Skill-facing and template docs were checked where they instruct agents how to render optional templates and scaffold through Level contracts.
Reserved for cross-runtime mirror consistency iteration.

findingDetails.title: Validation references are not registry-complete for the current validator surface
findingDetails.evidence: validation_rules.md claims complete coverage for every validation rule but its rule summary and path_scoped_rules.md list a legacy subset, while validate.sh derives default rules from scripts/lib/validator-registry.json, which includes additional enforced rules such as TEMPLATE_SOURCE, TEMPLATE_HEADERS, SECTION_COUNTS, and SPEC_DOC_INTEGRITY.
findingDetails.recommendation: Generate or verify validation references from scripts/lib/validator-registry.json, or add a test asserting validation_rules.md and path_scoped_rules.md include all default non-skip registry rules with matching severity/category labels.
findingDetails.scopeProof: validate.sh loads scripts/lib/validator-registry.json and enumerates non-skip default rules; direct doc reads show the validation references describe only a subset of those registry entries.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Validation references were compared against validate.sh, the registry, representative rule scripts, and validator/template tests.
Relevant validator and template tests were inspected for coverage shape; no direct docs-vs-registry parity test was found in inspected surfaces.
SKILL.md exposes the current validation command and exit-code taxonomy while detailed rule coverage is delegated to validation references.
Reserved for cross-runtime mirror consistency iteration.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 validator-coverage finding was found; weighted new findings ratio is 1/1.
edgeCases: Direct artifact writing was unavailable/disabled; command manager should materialize the returned recovery-ready markdown and JSONL.
Dispatch listed check-* implementation paths under scripts/spec, but live validator registry resolves those rule scripts under scripts/rules; treated as scope-preserving path correction.
Prior P2-001 and P2-002 were not duplicated.
No P0/P1 opened because runtime validation remains registry-driven; the risk is stale coverage guidance rather than a validation bypass.
ruledOut: No finding opened for dispatch path typo because registry-resolved live rule paths were reviewed.
No cross-runtime mirror finding attempted in this dimension.

findingDetails.title: Iteration prompt pack points executor contexts at a non-existent review doctrine path
findingDetails.evidence: The shared prompt pack tells executors to load `.agents/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`, while the canonical agent loads `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`; the YAML renders the prompt pack and passes it to native and CLI executor routes.
findingDetails.recommendation: Change the prompt-pack doctrine path to `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`, or render a runtime-resolved variable that always points to an existing review_core.md surface before dispatch.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The prompt pack is rendered by deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml and used as native context and CLI Codex stdin; direct file discovery found the canonical .opencode review_core path and no .agents/skills review_core path.

findingDetails.title: Runtime mirrors re-label their packaged mirror path as canonical while command/YAML declares `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` canonical
findingDetails.evidence: The canonical OpenCode agent says path references should use `.opencode/agents/*.md`, and the auto workflow names `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` as the canonical path; mirror files instead state their own runtime directory as the canonical runtime path reference while also saying mirrors are downstream packaging surfaces.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reword mirror Path Convention lines to state that the packaged runtime file is a mirror and `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` remains the canonical source/reference path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads across all four agent definitions show the same contract body and mirror-awareness table, with only the Path Convention line varying by runtime while YAML marks `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` canonical.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Compared canonical OpenCode agent, Claude mirror, Codex TOML mirror, and Gemini mirror; one P2 wording drift found.
Checked sk-deep-review skill and prompt-pack executor guidance; one P1 non-existent review_core path found.
Checked command/YAML canonical agent path, prompt-pack render/use, executor routes, and artifact validation requirements.
Loaded `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md` before severity classification.

noveltyJustification: Two new findings were found in the final cross-runtime pass. Weighted new findings are P1=5 plus P2=1; all weighted findings in this iteration are new, so ratio is 6/6 = 1.0.
edgeCases: Direct artifact writing was unavailable/disabled; command manager should materialize returned recovery-ready markdown, state JSONL, delta JSONL, and strategy update summary.

Strategy contains stale machine-owned blocked entries for earlier deferred agent_cross_runtime passes; active Next Focus and dispatch explicitly required cross-runtime-mirror-consistency, so this iteration proceeded.
P2-004 was kept advisory because YAML still names `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` as canonical.
P1-001 was classified required because the shared prompt pack references a non-existent doctrine path used by executor routes.
ruledOut: No delta-file omission finding opened because YAML and prompt pack both require the delta artifact.
No duplicate finding opened for prior P2-001, P2-002, or P2-003.
No finding opened for the runtime mirror awareness table itself; mirror read-only/downstream wording is consistent.
integrationEvidence: .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:666-680 native and CLI executor prompt use
